source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/00_기타 작업 서류/240718 - DK 제품별 자료 정리/DK_제품별 자료 정리(pdf)/4. A1417MCW/6. IEC Test Report (EMC, Safety)/PRJ0042257 REP028549 CBTR IEC60601-1 H&abyz A1717MCW (Original)_CSV.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: IEC 60601-1 test report excerpt for a power supply unit, covering clauses on fluid ingress, hazardous situations, single-fault conditions, PEMS, construction, transformers, ME systems, EMC, and flammable anesthetic hazards.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports safety and EMC regulatory evidence by documenting applicable, non-applicable, and passed IEC 60601-1 clause assessments for the evaluated equipment and noting items requiring end-product evaluation.
keyPoints: The equipment is repeatedly identified as a power supply unit requiring additional evaluation in the end-product application for several construction, enclosure, system, and usability-related clauses.
Interruption and restoration of power supply did not create a hazard and received a passing verdict.
Hazardous situation and single-fault assessments passed for emissions, enclosure deformation, temperature limits, leakage current, accessible-part voltage, and applied single-fault combinations.

The equipment has no programmable electrical medical system, no alarm system, no batteries, no heating elements, no motors, no liquid reservoir, and is not intended for sterilization or flammable anesthetic use.
Mains supply transformer overheating, short-circuit, overload, and dielectric strength related checks passed by reference to appended tables.

entities.name: Report No. 269183
entities.evidence: Pages 70-92 identify the document as Report No. 269183 under IEC 60601-1.

entities.name: IEC 60601-1
entities.evidence: Each page header states IEC 60601-1 with clause requirements, test results, remarks, and verdicts.

entities.name: TRF No. IEC60601_1J
entities.evidence: The page headers list TRF No. IEC60601_1J.

entities.name: ISO 14971
entities.evidence: Multiple clauses reference ISO 14971 Clauses 4.2-4.4, 5, and 6.2-6.5 for risk management file assessments.

entities.name: IEC 62304
entities.evidence: Clause 14.1 references software classification, development, risk management, configuration management, and problem resolution clauses of IEC 62304.

entities.name: Power supply unit
entities.evidence: Several remarks state: 'The equipment is a power supply unit, shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application.'

concepts.name: End-product application evaluation
concepts.evidence: The report repeatedly states the equipment is a power supply unit and shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application.

concepts.name: Interruption and restoration of power supply
concepts.evidence: Clause 11.8 states interruption and restoration of power supply did not result in loss of basic safety or essential performance; remark: no hazard associated with interruption of power; verdict P.

concepts.name: Hazardous situations and single fault conditions
concepts.evidence: Clause 13 reports passing verdicts for specific hazardous situations, leakage current in single fault condition, accessible-part voltage limits, and single fault conditions.

concepts.name: PEMS non-applicability
concepts.evidence: Clause 14 states no programmable electrical medical system and marks PEMS software lifecycle, validation, verification, IT-network, and modification requirements as N/A.

concepts.name: Transformer overheating protection
concepts.evidence: Clause 15.5.1 states transformers are protected against overheating, with short-circuit, overload, and dielectric strength tests referenced to appended tables and verdict P.

concepts.name: Flammable anesthetic non-use
concepts.evidence: Clauses 11.4 and G.2.1 state the equipment is not intended for use with flammable anesthetics and is not category AP or APG equipment.

evidence.claim: The equipment is not intended for use with flammable anesthetics or flammable agents.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.4 remark: 'Not intended for use with flammable anesthetics.' Clause 11.5 remark: 'Not intended for use in conjunction with flammable agents.'

evidence.claim: The equipment does not contain a liquid reservoir and does not require handling liquids in normal use.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.6.1 states the equipment does not contain a liquid reservoir; Clause 11.6.3 states it does not use liquids and is not required to handle liquids in normal use.

evidence.claim: Interruption and restoration of the power supply did not result in loss of basic safety or essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 11.8: 'No hazard associated with interruption of power.' Verdict P.

evidence.claim: The equipment has no alarm system under IEC 60601-1-8 applicability.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 12.3 states manufacturer implemented an alarm system compliant with IEC 60601-1-8; remark: 'No alarm systems.' Verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The equipment does not produce X-radiation, radiotherapy output, or other diagnostic or therapeutic radiation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clauses 12.4.5.2 to 12.4.5.4 state the equipment does not produce X-radiation, is not radiotherapy equipment, and does not produce diagnostic or therapeutic radiation.

evidence.claim: Hazardous situation tests passed for emissions and enclosure deformation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 13.1.2 states no emission of flames, molten metal, poisonous or ignitable substance, and no enclosure deformation; both verdicts P.

evidence.claim: Single-fault leakage current and accessible-part voltage limits passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 13.1.3 states leakage current limits in single fault condition did not exceed appended Table 8.7 and accessible-part voltage limits did not exceed clause 8.4.2; verdicts P.

evidence.claim: The equipment passed the general single fault condition assessment.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 13.2.1 states normal conditions in least favourable combination applied during single fault conditions and references appended Table 13.2; verdict P.

evidence.claim: The equipment has no PEMS and Clause 14 PEMS requirements were treated as not applicable.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 14.1 remark: 'No programmable electrical medical system. The equipment is a power supply unit, shall be additionally evaluated in the end product.' Verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The equipment has no batteries or replaceable battery system.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.4.3.1 states 'No batteries'; Clause 15.4.3.2 states 'No replaceable battery.'

evidence.claim: Indicator light requirements for readiness and color compliance passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.4.4 references clause 7.8 for indicator lights and states colors of indicator lights complied with 7.8.1; verdicts P.

evidence.claim: Mains supply transformer overheating protection passed.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.5.1 states transformers are protected against overheating, windings did not open, no hazardous situation occurred, winding temperatures did not exceed Table 31, and dielectric strength testing was conducted after short-circuit and overload tests; verdicts P.

evidence.claim: Transformer output winding short-circuit and multiple overload testing passed by appended table reference.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 15.5.1.2 references appended Table 15.5.1.2 with verdict P, and Clause 15.5.1.3 references appended Table 15.5.1.3 with verdict P.

evidence.claim: ME system requirements are generally not applicable because the equipment is a power supply unit requiring end-product evaluation.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 16.1 states the equipment is a power supply unit and shall be additionally evaluated in the end product application; ME system subclauses are marked N/A.

evidence.claim: EMC risks were not evaluated in this IEC 60601-1 report and are to be determined in the end product.
evidence.evidenceText: Clause 17 remark: 'Not considered EMC. Compliance in this test report; To be determined in the end product.' Verdict N/A.

evidence.claim: The equipment is not category AP or APG equipment for flammable anesthetic mixture protection.
evidence.evidenceText: Annex G Clause G.2.1 remark: 'No category AP and APG equipment.' Verdict N/A.

reviewFlags.reason: This is a partial chunk from pages 70-92 of 138; title page, date, manufacturer, and complete model information are not present in the chunk.
Many clauses are marked N/A because the evaluated item is a power supply unit and must be additionally evaluated in the end-product application.
Several compliance claims reference appended tables that are not included in this chunk, including Tables 8.7, 8.8.3, 13.2, 15.5.1.2, and 15.5.1.3.
